zh.lifeSummary: 當Gruffudd ap Llywelyn of Wales於大約1284年出生在英國威爾斯時，他的父親Llywelyn ap Madog36歲，母親Mrs. Llywelyn ap Madog28歲。他去世於1326年享年43歲，埋葬在英國威爾斯丹比爾利藍希利奧。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Gruffudd ap Llywelyn of Wales于大约1284年出生在英国威尔斯时，他的父亲Llywelyn ap Madog36岁，母亲Mrs. Llywelyn ap Madog28岁。他去世于1326年享年43岁，埋葬在英国威尔斯丹比爾利藍希利奧。

nameMeanings.name: Gruffudd
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Welsh Grip(p)iud, from an unexplained first element + a second element meaning ‘lord, prince’. Gruffydd ap Llewelyn ( d. 1063 ) was one of the most able rulers of Wales in the Middle Ages, scoring notable victories over the English until he was eventually defeated by King Harold in 1063 .

variants.name: Griffudd
meanings: From Old Welsh Grip(p)iud, from an unexplained first element + a second element meaning ‘lord, prince’. Gruffydd ap Llewelyn ( d. 1063 ) was one of the most able rulers of Wales in the Middle Ages, scoring notable victories over the English until he was eventually defeated by King Harold in 1063 .

variants.name: Gruffud
meanings: From Old Welsh Grip(p)iud, from an unexplained first element + a second element meaning ‘lord, prince’. Gruffydd ap Llewelyn ( d. 1063 ) was one of the most able rulers of Wales in the Middle Ages, scoring notable victories over the English until he was eventually defeated by King Harold in 1063 .

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

nameMeanings.name: Llywelyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Llewelyn
meanings: Traditional: altered form (influenced by llew ‘lion’) of Llywelyn, an ancient name of uncertain derivation. It probably goes back to the Old Celtic name Lugobelinos, the first element of which is Lugu- (the name of a god; see Lleu ); the second is a name-forming element found also in names such as Cunobelinus (Cymbeline). The name was borne by Llywelyn ap Iorwerth ( 1173–1240 ) and his grandson Llywelyn ap Gruffydd ( d. 1282 ), Welsh princes who for a time united their countrymen in North Wales and led opposition to the power of the Norman barons in South Wales and the Marches.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Wales
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from Wales near Sheffield (South Yorkshire). The placename means ‘The Welshmen’ and denoted a settlement of Brittonic speakers living on the boundary between the Anglo-Saxon kingdoms of Northumbria and Mercia (from Old English wēalas, walas, plural of walh, wealh, originally meaning ‘Roman citizen’ and referring to the native British population, but in Old English this term later came to mean ‘serf, unfree person, foreigner’ or ‘Welshman’). See also Wallis .
habitational name from Wales, the country named from Old English wēalas ‘foreigners’ (especially those speaking Celtic or Romance languages). This meaning of the surname is quite rare.

variants.name: Wallis
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which is a derivative of   William .

en.lifeSummary: When Gruffudd ap Llywelyn of Wales was born about 1284, in Wales, United Kingdom, his father, Llywelyn ap Madog, was 36 and his mother, Mrs. Llywelyn ap Madog, was 28. He died in 1326, at the age of 43, and was buried in Llantysilio, Denbighshire, Wales, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gruffudd ap Llywelyn of Wales est né environ 1284, à Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Llywelyn ap Madog, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Llywelyn ap Madog, avait 28 ans. Il est décédé en 1326, à l’âge de 43 ans, et a été enterré à Llantysilio, Denbighshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Gruffudd ap Llywelyn of Wales um 1284 in Wales, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Llywelyn ap Madog 36 und seine Mutter Mrs. Llywelyn ap Madog 28. Er starb 1326 im Alter von 43 Jahren und wurde in Llantysilio, Denbighshire, Wales, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Gruffudd ap Llywelyn of Wales è nato il circa 1284 nel Galles, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Llywelyn ap Madog, aveva 36 anni e il suo madre, Mrs. Llywelyn ap Madog, aveva 28 anni. Morì nel 1326, all'età di 43 anni, e fu sepolto a Llantysilio, Denbighshire, Galles, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Gruffudd ap Llywelyn of Walesが約1284年にイギリスウェールズで生まれた時，父Llywelyn ap Madogは36歳，母Mrs. Llywelyn ap Madogは28歳であった。彼は1326年に43歳で死去し，イギリスウェールズデンビグhシュアイアラントイシリオにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Gruffudd ap Llywelyn of Wales(이)가 대략 1284년 영국 웨일스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Llywelyn ap Madog(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Llywelyn ap Madog(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1326년 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 웨일스 덴비그h실르 랜티실리오에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Gruffudd ap Llywelyn of Wales nasceu aproximadamente 1284, em Gales, United Kingdom, seu pai, Llywelyn ap Madog, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Llywelyn ap Madog, tinha 28 anos. Ele faleceu em 1326, com 43 anos, e foi sepultado em Llantysilio, Denbighshire, Gales, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gruffudd ap Llywelyn of Wales родился около 1284 г. в местности Уэльс, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Llywelyn ap Madog было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Llywelyn ap Madog, 28. Он умер в 1326 г. в возрасте 43 лет и был похоронен в местности ллантисилио, денбаишир, Уэльс, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Gruffudd ap Llywelyn of Wales nació aproximadamente 1284, en Gales, Reino Unido, su papá, Llywelyn ap Madog, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Mrs. Llywelyn ap Madog, tenía 28 años. Murió en 1326, a la edad de 43 años, y fue sepultado en Llantysilio, Denbighshire, Gales, Reino Unido.
